items.title: NT Supreme Court clears way for potential wave of summary adjournments

children.content: The Northern Territory Supreme Court has cleared the way for criminal defendants refused legal aid on funding grounds to have their cases adjourned indefinitely by overturning its own two-decade old ruling.

gallery.altText: a woman wearing glasses and a paisley dress sitting in a dark room

thumbnail.altText: a woman wearing glasses and a paisley dress sitting in a dark room

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

items.title: Cost-of-living pressure 'entrenching disadvantage' in the NT

children.content: The NT has the second highest average rent in the country, with households having to set aside more of their weekly wages to cover essential costs.

image.altText: Aerial shot of houses in Darwin

items.title: Under-fire CEO defends Alice Springs camp 'squalor' after Kumanjayi Little Baby's death

children.content: After the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the state of Alice Springs's town camps shocked the nation. Now, the man in charge of servicing them is speaking out.

gallery.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

thumbnail.altText: A love heart is drawn on a road into some gates.

items.title: Tour operators brace for Kakadu site closures 'like COVID all over again'

children.content: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

image.altText: An aerial shot of Jim Jim Falls

items.title: Dramatic daylight arrest of NT fugitive, with police shooting car tyre

children.content: Northern Territory police have carried out a dramatic arrest of a 33-year-old man on a main Darwin road, saying the fugitive was wanted for a breach of parole.

image.altText: A police car blocking traffic on a main road in a tropical suburban area.

items.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

items.title: Border Force busts NT multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation

children.content: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

image.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

items.title: NT scrutiny committee members call for shake-up after recommendations rejected

children.content: Non-government members of the Northern Territory Legislative Scrutiny Committee are calling for the body to be reformed, saying it is not operating the way it was intended.

gallery.altText: A parliamentary committee hears evidence from witnesses

thumbnail.altText: A parliamentary committee hears evidence from witnesses

items.title: Almost one-third of NT children in care allegedly harmed, report finds

children.content: Almost one-third of children in out-of-home care in the Northern Territory were allegedly harmed in the last financial year, according to a new report from the Office of the Children's Commissioner.

image.altText: Children's toys, shoes, coloring pens.

items.title: Dieback disease killing mango trees is spreading in the NT

children.content: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

image.altText: Needles injected into mango trees

items.title: Chinese spies using LinkedIn to steal secrets from recruits, Five Eyes warns

children.content: Australia and its partners in the Five Eyes intelligence alliance warn that Chinese spies are using job platforms to post fake ads to lure recruits with access to sensitive information.

image.altText: A screenshot of several apps on a screen, including LinkedIn.

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

image.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: The streaming platform exploding in popularity

children.content: Whether or not you've heard of the livestreaming platform Kick, there’s a good chance you’ll have seen clips from it.
Kick has become home to some of the internet's most controversial creators — from celebrities and manosphere influencers to looksmaxxers.
But what is Kick, and why has it become such a major player in livestreaming?

image.altText: A person in a dark shirt gestures while speaking against a soft purple backdrop. Bold text reads, "WHAT'S THE APPEAL OF KICK?"

items.title: RBA governor says 'practically no-one is in negative equity' — as it happened

children.content: RBA Governor Michele Bullock has told Senate estimates that "practically no-one is in negative equity"
Meanwhile, the ASX remains in the red.

image.altText: An ASX board shot by John Gunn in Deccember 2022.

items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

image.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

items.title: Teen's brutal death a flashpoint over violence against women and femicide

children.content: The alleged brutal murder of 14-year-old Agostina Vega in Argentina has reignited a generation-defining movement demanding justice for the women victims of crime and violence.

gallery.altText: A dark-haired female protester holding placards with the greyscale face of a teenage girl with the words "Justice For Agostina".

thumbnail.altText: A dark-haired female protester holding placards with the greyscale face of a teenage girl with the words "Justice For Agostina".

items.title: Sydney nurses' alleged threats 'stupid things said by stupid people', court told

children.content: A lawyer acting for one of two Sydney nurses accused of making antisemitic threats via an online video chat app has told a court their actions were "stupid things said by stupid people".

image.altText: Ahmad Rashad Nadir and Sarah Abu Lebdeh both wearing sunglasses.

items.title: White Australia's bid to remove hate listing dismissed

children.content: The High Court has dismissed an application by the group White Australia which had hoped to lift its listing as a hate group ahead of a formal High Court hearing later in the year.

image.altText: The front of a courthouse

items.title: What happens when a Restoration Australia project takes five years

children.content: A planned 12-month build that's still underway three years later is the running joke of design shows. But what happens when you're a TV producer trying to make a series of it?

Anthony Burke wears a suit, smiling.

items.title: Big spending on health and housing in 'no surprises' SA budget

children.content: South Australia's debt is projected to hit to more than $50 billion by 2029-30, with health taking up about one third of the budget.

image.altText: Tom Koutsantonis is standing behind a lecturn and speaking into a microphone.

items.title: Who gets a public holiday on Monday?

children.content: Many Australians are about to have a long weekend thanks to the King's Birthday public holiday on Monday. But not everyone will have the day off.

image.altText: A close up of King Charles wearing a grey suit with a dark pink tie

items.title: Motorcyclist says he owes his life to bystanders who lifted car off him

children.content: Tyler Weibe was on his way to work when the crash occurred on Brisbane's north side, leaving him with broken ribs, a broken collarbone and a collapsed lung.

children.content: Experts say healing might involve reducing emotional distress and gaining clarity from the situation.

items.title: EPA clashes with Greens on need for real-time tracking of salmon antibiotic use

children.content: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

image.altText: A woman with glasses leans forwards and speaks into a microphone in a committee room.

items.title: What's got the Pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new AI technology

children.content: Most Australians appear to distrust and dislike artificial intelligence despite using technology more than people in other countries.

articles.title: Australia joins exclusive club granting access to advanced AI

children.content: Fresh access to AI deemed too dangerous for public release has ramped up pressure on the government to respond to possible risks.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

articles.title: Economic report says 82 occupations have been 'AI-disrupted'

children.content: A new economic report by consultancy firm Deloitte says the AI "winners" will be organisations that combine human and machine strengths, with recruiters saying human judgement will still be key.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt and long hair looks at camera.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of Pope Leo.

items.title: Boxing trainer suspended after neo-Nazi Sewell trained at his gym

children.content: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell has been training at a boxing gym in the outer-Melbourne suburb of Diamond Creek, a short drive from where he is living. The gym has been suspended by the sport's governing body.

image.altText: A bald man with a moustache in the foreground superimposed over a blurred boxing ring

items.title: Neo-Nazi hecklers found guilty over Anzac Day booing

children.content: Three white supremacists who loudly booed during a Welcome to Country ceremony at an Anzac Day dawn service in Melbourne have been found guilty of offensive behaviour and ordered to pay fines.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit and white shirt carrying a book walks outside a court room.

items.title: Pentagon hires convicted January 6 rioter for national security job

children.content: The Trump administration has given a man who was jailed for his actions during the January 6, 2021, US Capitol riot a job in the Pentagon's policy office.

image.altText: A young man in a red MAGA hat and red jumper, looking over a wall and holding what appears to be a metal pole

items.title: Israel and Lebanon renew ceasefire but Hezbollah not present at talks

children.content: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to a ceasefire, according to a joint statement with the United States.

image.altText: Smoke billows over buildings in southern Lebanon after a missile strike

items.title: Pat Cummins hoping to stay fit for 'unprecedented' 21-Test stretch

children.content: Australian men's captain Pat Cummins says his body is feeling "awesome" ahead of a hectic 12-month stretch of Test cricket.

items.title: MP uses parliament to reveal threats to NZYQ deportees in Nauru

children.content: A cohort of former detainees deported to Nauru by the federal government have faced threats of violence and verbal abuse, independent MP Andrew Wilkie has revealed under parliamentary privilege.

items.title: Far South Coast breaks records in oyster growing turnaround

children.content: While oyster growers have had a tough time recently, one lake on the NSW Far South Coast is bucking the trend and setting new records along the way.

image.altText: Oyster farmer in an estaury.

items.title: Meta hits out at Labor's plan to make tech giants pay for news

children.content: Tech giant Meta criticises the Australian government's plan to make social media companies pay for news, calling it a "grossly unfair" and "discriminatory tax".

image.altText: Three hands in silhouette in front of Meta logo.

items.title: Life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug to be pulled from shelves

children.content: Patients have been left frustrated and scared after learning that a life-saving breast cancer and endometriosis drug is to be pulled from shelves in Australia and some other countries.

image.altText: A box of Zoladex 3.6mg.
